title: Heterogeneous structure of the Northern Chile marine forearc and its implications for megathrust earthquakes
abstract: A density-depth model along the Nazca-South America subduction margin from 18°S to 23°30′S is constrained with seismic reflection profiles, published velocity-depth tomographic models, and relocated seismicity. The findings reveal a heterogeneous structure for the Northern Chile marine forearc with latitudinal and longitudinal segmentation of continental wedge properties. Changes in density can be explained by changes in fracturing degree, which could have an important control on the Iquique earthquake rupture process. Seismic reflection data integrated in this study are from prior surveys offshore northern Chile.

location.description: Northern Chilean marine forearc between 18°S and 23°30′S, integrating published seismic data for density modelling

data.name: Published seismic reflection profiles and velocity models (18°–23.5°S, north Chile)
data.description: Compilation of published marine seismic reflection profiles and velocity-depth tomographic models from prior CINCA and BGR surveys offshore northern Chile (18°–23.5°S), used to constrain a density-depth model.

processing.summary: Gravity and density modelling study using published seismic reflection profiles and velocity models from prior surveys (CINCA and BGR experiments) offshore northern Chile (18°–23.5°S). No new seismic data acquired. The density model is constrained by published velocity-depth models, seismic reflection profiles, and relocated seismicity. Results reveal latitudinal and longitudinal segmentation of the northern Chilean forearc.
workflow: 1. Compilation of published seismic reflection profiles and velocity models
3. Density-depth model construction from velocity-density conversion
processing.notes: Primarily a modelling paper using published seismic data. Universidad de Chile + IRD (France) collaboration. No new seismic acquisition.

analysis_notes: Paper behind paywall in GJI; no open access version found. Abstract reconstructed from survey snippet and Semantic Scholar metadata. Primarily a modelling study using published seismic data as input. No new seismic surveys reported. Bonvalot (IRD, France) co-authorship confirms French-Chilean collaboration.
